title: Feistel round template

holes.name: ROUND_KEY_TYPE
holes.description: Type of one round key, e.g. [48]

holes.name: HALF_TYPE
holes.description: Type of one state half, e.g. [32]

template_code:
round : (ROUND_KEY_TYPE, [2]HALF_TYPE) -> [2]HALF_TYPE
round (k, [l, r]) = [r, l ^ F_FUNC (r, k)]
usage_notes: Use when modeling Feistel-family ciphers such as DES-like designs.
Switch to concatenation form `r # (...)` if the surrounding code expects a flat block instead of `[2]HALF_TYPE`.
Usually paired with a final half-swap before the final permutation.
retrieval_text: Template for a Feistel round. Takes a round key and two state halves, returns swapped halves with XOR against a round function output.

title: Apply a normalized permutation table with @@
template_code:
permute : INPUT_TYPE -> OUTPUT_TYPE
permute xs = xs @@ TABLE_NAME
usage_notes: Normalize specification tables to zero-based indices before using `@@`.
Works for reordering, expansion, compression, and selection tables.
Best paired with a nearby constant-table entry so the table itself is also retrievable.
retrieval_text: Template for applying a normalized permutation table in Cryptol: xs @@ TABLE_NAME. Suitable for DES/AES-style reordering, expansion, or compression steps.

title: Convert 1-based specification indices to 0-based Cryptol indices
template_code:
zeroBasify xs = [ i - 1 | i <- xs ]

NORMALIZED_NAME = zeroBasify TABLE_NAME
usage_notes: Very common when porting textbook or standards permutation tables into Cryptol.
Use the normalized table only for Cryptol indexing operators like `@`, `@@`, `!`, or `!!`.
Keep the original table as a separate artifact if traceability back to the standard matters.
retrieval_text: Template for converting 1-based specification tables to 0-based Cryptol index tables using a comprehension that subtracts one from each entry.

title: Scheduled rotations plus compression-selection key schedule

holes.name: ROUND_KEY_TYPE
holes.description: Type of one derived round key

template_code:
expand : [2]HALF_TYPE -> [ROUNDS]ROUND_KEY_TYPE
expand [kl, kr] =
    [ ((kl <<< i) # (kr <<< i)) @@ COMPRESSION_TABLE
    | i <- sums SHIFT_SCHEDULE ]

sums xs = ys
  where ys = [ x + y | x <- xs | y <- [0] # ys ]
usage_notes: Good for DES-style key schedules with cumulative rotation amounts.
Replace `#` plus `@@` with any other combine/select step that matches the target algorithm.
The `sums` helper is a reusable recurrence template for cumulative offsets.
retrieval_text: Template for a DES-style key schedule: cumulative shifts, rotate two halves with <<<, concatenate, then select/compress with a table.

title: Chunked S-box pipeline with per-chunk numbered dispatch
template_code:
applyBoxes : INPUT_TYPE -> OUTPUT_TYPE
applyBoxes x =
    join [ SINGLE_BOX_FUNC (n, chunk)
         | n     <- [1 .. BOX_COUNT]
         | chunk <- split x ]
usage_notes: Use when an input block is partitioned into fixed-size chunks and each chunk is transformed by a numbered lookup table.
For DES-style row/column indexing, define a second helper that slices outer and inner bits from each chunk.
If the target design uses one shared box instead of numbered boxes, remove the `n` parameter.
retrieval_text: Template for chunked substitution pipelines: split an input block into chunks, dispatch each chunk to a numbered box function, then join outputs.
